sourceTitle: Hosting job listings and collecting applications: A Notion guide for startups

questions.question: What is the primary benefit of using Notion to create a jobs page for a startup?

options.text: It allows you to store and run backend server code.
options.explanation: Notion is not a server‑side platform; it is a content‑creation and publishing tool.

options.text: You can build a jobs page quickly without needing engineering support.
options.explanation: The guide emphasizes that Notion lets non‑technical teams create and publish job listings without a developer.

options.text: It automatically posts jobs to all major job boards.
options.explanation: Notion does not push listings to external job boards; you must share the published URL yourself.

options.text: It creates a native mobile app for recruiting.
options.explanation: Notion publishes a web page, not a dedicated mobile application.

questions.question: When you edit a job listing in your Notion workspace, how does the change appear on the published web page?

options.text: You must manually republish the page each time.
options.explanation: The guide states that changes update in real time; no manual republish is needed.

options.text: The change updates automatically and appears instantly on the web page.
options.explanation: Notion syncs the published version with the workspace in real time.

options.text: Updates are queued and appear after a 24‑hour delay.
options.explanation: There is no built‑in delay; updates are immediate.

options.text: Only text edits are reflected; media must be re‑uploaded.
options.explanation: All content types, including images and embeds, update in real time.

questions.question: Which of the following content types cannot be directly added to a Notion job listing page?

options.text: Headers
options.explanation: Headers are a standard block type in Notion and can be added.

options.text: Callouts
options.explanation: Callouts are a native Notion block, usable in any page.

options.text: Embedded Greenhouse page
options.explanation: The guide specifically mentions embedding a Greenhouse page via the Create embed option.

options.text: Server‑side scripts
options.explanation: Notion does not execute custom server‑side code; it only hosts static content blocks.

questions.question: To maintain brand coherence on a Notion jobs page, which design elements are recommended?

options.text: Page icons, covers, and colored backgrounds
options.explanation: The article lists these visual elements as ways to align the page with your brand.

options.text: Only plain text without any visual styling
options.explanation: Plain text lacks visual branding; the guide encourages richer design.

options.text: Changing the page’s URL to include keywords
options.explanation: While useful for SEO, URL changes are not a visual branding element discussed in the guide.

options.text: Adding a chatbot widget
options.explanation: Chatbots are not mentioned as a built‑in Notion feature for job pages.

questions.question: If you want each job listing to have its own detailed page, what Notion feature should you use?

options.text: Create a subpage for each listing
options.explanation: The guide explains that each listing can be turned into a separate page for more details.

options.text: Only use an inline table without subpages
options.explanation: An inline table holds data but does not automatically give a full page for each entry.

options.text: Link to external websites for each job
options.explanation: While possible, the guide focuses on keeping details inside Notion subpages.

options.text: Attach a PDF file to each row in a database
options.explanation: PDF attachments are not the same as a dedicated Notion page with its own layout.

questions.question: How can you create columns on a Notion jobs page?

options.text: Write custom HTML code in a code block
options.explanation: Notion does not allow raw HTML for layout; columns are built with drag‑and‑drop.

options.text: Drag and drop content blocks side‑by‑side
options.explanation: The guide states that you can create columns by dragging blocks with the mouse.

options.text: Insert a spreadsheet and enable column view
options.explanation: Spreadsheets display data but do not create visual page columns.

options.text: Install a third‑party plugin that adds column functionality
options.explanation: Column creation is a native Notion interaction; no plugin is required.

questions.question: When embedding a Greenhouse job posting using Notion’s "Create embed" option, which condition is essential for the embed to work on the published web page?

options.text: The Greenhouse link must be publicly accessible.
options.explanation: If the source URL is behind a login or private, the embed cannot render for external visitors.

options.text: The embed must be set to "private" in Notion.
options.explanation: Making an embed private would hide it from the public page, defeating the purpose.

options.text: The Notion page itself must be password protected.
options.explanation: Password protection would restrict public access to the entire page, including the embed.

options.text: The embed must be uploaded as a PDF file.
options.explanation: Embeds work with live URLs; a PDF is a static file and not a live embed.

questions.question: You have already published a Notion jobs page. After updating the cover image in the workspace, what must you do for the new cover to appear on the public version?

options.text: Republish the page via the Share menu.
options.explanation: Re‑publishing is unnecessary because Notion syncs changes automatically.

options.text: Do nothing; the change appears automatically.
options.explanation: The guide specifies that updates are reflected in real time on the web page.

options.text: Delete the public page and create a new one.
options.explanation: Deleting and recreating is excessive; the system handles updates without this step.

options.text: Ask every visitor to clear their browser cache.
options.explanation: While caching can affect what a user sees, the page itself updates instantly; users don’t need to clear caches.
